profile.name: Brian Matoke
profile.tagline: I build AI systems that work in the real world.
bio: I design and ship systems that sit at the intersection of machine learning, data engineering, and the governance that lets both be trusted in production. That spans multi-agent and RAG architectures, models hardened against adversarial attack, ETL pipelines that hold up at tens-of-millions-of-records scale, and — as a named Data Protection Officer — the audit trails and privacy controls that let all of it legally touch real people's data.

Started by digitising 20M+ land records for Kenya's national land-mapping programme, went to Hungary to study exactly how models fail under adversarial attack, and came back to keep building — now shipping AI automation platforms and open tools for Kenyan researchers, businesses, and developers.

languages.note: Computed from the GitHub API's per-repo language byte counts, summed across non-fork repositories.

projects.name: MedLit Search
projects.summary: Clinical literature search across 6 free databases simultaneously, with one-click routed access to 8 paywalled databases and AI-powered evidence synthesis.
projects.problem: researchers and clinicians juggle six or more separate database search interfaces and manually re-run the same query on each one, then manually work out which results they can actually reach through their institution's paywall access.
projects.approach: a single-file React app that queries PubMed, Europe PMC, Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, CrossRef, and DOAJ in parallel, auto-detects study type, offers a structured PICO search mode, checks Unpaywall for legal open-access copies, and — with the visitor's own Anthropic API key — asks Claude to synthesise up to 20 papers into an evidence-quality summary.
projects.outcome: a zero-install, zero-cost tool that ships as one static HTML file on GitHub Pages.
projects.stack: React 18 (CDN), Babel Standalone, PubMed / Europe PMC / Semantic Scholar / OpenAlex / CrossRef / DOAJ / Unpaywall APIs, Claude

projects.name: DailyStack
projects.summary: Offline-first habit and life tracker with SMART goals, analytics, and time-gated reminders.
projects.problem: most habit trackers need a constant connection and stop being useful the moment daily life gets in the way.
projects.approach: an offline-first tracker with SMART goals and analytics that work whether or not you're online.
projects.outcome: built for actually sticking with a habit, not just logging one entry.

projects.name: ZUIO
projects.summary: A Kenyan word-guessing party game — describe the word without using any of the banned words.
projects.problem: wanted a lightweight, distinctly Kenyan party game friends could play in a browser.
projects.approach: describe the word, avoid the banned list, score points before time runs out.
projects.outcome: a fun, shareable local twist on a classic format.

projects.name: Pesa AI Logger
projects.summary: Local-first M-Pesa financial intelligence. Parses transaction SMS, categorises spending automatically.
projects.problem: Kenyans run their financial lives through M-Pesa but can't easily see spending patterns across scattered SMS.
projects.approach: a local Python/Flask parser with Polygon-blockchain tamper-evident logging, nothing sent to a third-party server.
projects.outcome: SMS history becomes a usable finance dashboard.

projects.name: ComplianceEngineMVP
projects.summary: Multi-tenant KDPA 2019 compliance automation with tamper-evident audit logging.
projects.problem: small Kenyan businesses can't afford consultants for KDPA 2019 compliance, so most skip it.
projects.approach: a FastAPI/Docker platform that scopes compliance via questionnaire and auto-generates documentation.
projects.outcome: compliance becomes achievable for businesses that would otherwise ignore it.

projects.name: Mail2Print
projects.summary: Unattended print automation daemon for Raspberry Pi and Linux.
projects.problem: unattended print stations need emailed PDFs printed without a human watching.
projects.approach: a daemon watching an inbox via IMAP IDLE, with duplicate detection and error recovery.
projects.outcome: email a PDF, and it's already printed.

projects.name: Sheria AI
projects.summary: RAG legal research system for Kenya — semantic search over case law and statutes.
projects.problem: Kenyan legal research is scattered with no semantic search.
projects.approach: FastAPI + pgvector + BGE-M3 embeddings + React, built alongside a research paper targeting JIPIT Strathmore.
projects.outcome: proof that RAG legal tools can be built for Kenyan law specifically.

thesis.title: Adaptive Adversarial Training — Interactive Demo
thesis.summary: MSc thesis, University of Szeged. A live simulation of the AAT feedback loop: per-class ε budgets that adapt to class robustness, Boltzmann norm scheduling over ℓ∞/ℓ2/ℓ1, and union robustness climbing from 40% to 49% over a static RAMP-style baseline. Press Train and play with the dials.

experience.role: AI Automation Lead & Data Protection Officer
points: Designed and deployed a production AI automation platform processing 100 documents/day with full data lineage and audit controls.
Served as the organisation's named Data Protection Officer under Kenya's DPA 2019, with zero data incidents through proactive governance architecture.
Held EU Foundation Certificate in Data Protection to ground the compliance work in recognised practice.
Designed Adaptive Adversarial Training, a curriculum-based robustness framework evaluated across three simultaneous threat models (L-inf, L2, L1) on CIFAR-10.
Improved union robustness by +3.5pp and clean accuracy by +6.9pp over the published RAMP baseline, on a single 12GB GPU.
Completed the EU Foundation Certificate in Data Protection alongside the degree.
Built and maintained ETL pipelines processing 20M+ digitised citizen land records for Kenya's Joint National and Resource Mapping (JNAM) programme.
Designed ingestion-stage validation that cut data error rates by 50%+.
Led a 12-person technical team delivering the pipeline end-to-end.

research.name: Adversarial Robustness Research
research.desc: Designed an Adaptive Adversarial Training framework at University of Szeged. +6.9pp clean accuracy (81.3%→88.2%) and +3.5pp union robustness over the published RAMP baseline across three simultaneous threat models (L-inf, L2, L1).

research.name: Data Systems at Scale
research.desc: Built and maintained ETL pipelines processing 20M+ digitised citizen land records for Kenya's Joint National and Resource Mapping (JNAM) programme. Ingestion-stage validation cut data error rates by 50%+.

research.name: AI Automation & Data Governance
research.desc: Designed and deployed a production AI automation platform at Tamu Group processing 100 documents/day with full data lineage and GDPR-aligned audit controls. Served as Data Protection Officer under Kenya DPA 2019.
research.meta: Tamu Group, 2025 · EU Foundation Certificate in Data Protection

education.name: EU Foundation Certificate in Data Protection

writing.text: Regular contributor to discussions on AI governance in Kenya and East Africa. Published analysis of Kenya's Artificial Intelligence Bill 2026. Active member of Effective Altruism Nairobi and volunteer at EAGx Berlin 2024.
